title: "Two Swimmers Wrestled On The Spar"
content:
Two swimmers wrestled on the spar
    Until the morning sun,
    When one turned smiling to the land.
    O God, the other one!

    The stray ships passing spied a face
    Upon the waters borne,
    With eyes in death still begging raised,
    And hands beseeching thrown.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in ""Two Swimmers Wrestled On The Spar""...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
